name: VEGAN PUERTO RICAN PASTELÓN
intro: Pastelón is a popular Puerto Rican dish made using sweet plantains, ground meat and mozzarella cheese. Think: lasagna, but much sweeter and more comforting. My family didn’t make this much, but I had it for the first time at my friend Melissa’s house during high school. Melissa and I had class together and worked in a neighborhood supermarket called Pioneer in Morris Park. Melissa and her family are from the Dominican Republic, and for dinner one day, they offered me pastelón. At the time, I had no idea what it was. Melissa explained it was made with sweet plantains and raisins. Although I thought it was good, the raisins made it too sweet for me. (I’m very much a savory girl.) I decided to make it without the raisins and loved how it turned out. The sweet plantains meshed into the savory meat (which I replace with walnuts and shiitake mushrooms), and the melted cheese between the layers makes it such a comforting treat. Keep in mind when picking the sweet plantain, the darker, the sweeter—just make sure it isn’t mushy.

ingredients.item: bags shredded vegan mozzarella cheese (I recommend Violife® brand)

method: Slice your yellow plantains the long way, into three or four thin slices per plantain, depending on how thick it is.

In a large, deep pot, heat your oil to 350°F (180°C). Working in batches, add about six slices of plantain and fry until fully cooked through. Transfer to a paper towel to drain. Repeat until all your plantains are fried.
Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C). In a medium bowl, stir together the tomato sauce and picadillo.

In an 8 x 8–inch (20 x 20–cm) baking dish, arrange a layer of fried plantain on the bottom, then layer with half of the picadillo mixture. Sprinkle one-third of the vegan mozzarella cheese on top. Follow this with two more layers, but do not put the picadillo mixture on the very top layer. Instead, add only the remaining cheese and pour the JUST Egg over the top of the pastelón and along the edges, to prevent the pastelón from drying out during baking, and for binding.
Cover with foil and bake for 30 minutes, then remove the foil and broil for an additional 6 to 7 minutes. Cut into eight slices and serve with white rice or by itself. ¡Buen provecho!
